prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on a nearly black frame, with only a faint television-style watermark visible in the lower-right corner.

It then cuts to a bright, white kitchen. Two people are moving around a central island or counter; one wears a red top. The kitchen has a large window with warm-colored panes or curtains, a stovetop on the island, and household objects on surrounding counters. The motion is blurred, suggesting quick activity in the kitchen.

A close-up follows of a woman holding a white trigger-spray bottle labeled “Direct.” The label is blue with a large white “Direct” name and a red/yellow arrow-like logo. She is shown in warm sunlight near a window, holding the bottle upright.

The ad then shows a close view of a countertop or stovetop area. A hand aims the spray trigger toward a small yellowish stain or spill near the edge of a stove. The stain appears greasy or food-like, but its exact substance is not identifiable from the frame. Another shot shows the bottle being held near a stovetop and pan, reinforcing the kitchen-cleaning setting.

A close-up isolates a yellow spill or smear on a light surface, with some surrounding clear liquid or droplets. This is followed by an extreme close-up of the product label. The visible text includes “Direct,” “Multi-Purpose Cleaner,” and a claim on the label reading “Cuts Tough Grease and Grime!” The Lysol name appears at the bottom of the label.

The setting then changes to another kitchen scene, darker and more warmly lit, with a large window in the background. Two people lean over a countertop; one holds the Direct spray bottle. There are seasonal-looking objects such as pumpkins or orange decorations on or near the counter. Small copyright text reading “© 1992 L&F Products” appears at the bottom left.

The commercial then returns to product-focused shots: a blurred close-up of the Direct bottle moving past the camera, followed by a stable product shot of the white trigger-spray bottle standing on a counter in front of a bright window with plants or colored objects in the background.

Near the end, the product line expands visually. A white Direct spray bottle appears alongside a larger blue refill pouch or container. The final frame presents both items clearly against a plain light background: the white trigger-spray bottle on the left and a blue “Direct” refill package on the right. The refill package prominently displays the word “Refill,” the Direct branding, and “Multi-Purpose Cleaner.”
